behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in segmentation logic itself (single parser boundary), not a cross-module handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess needed; in-memory input string reproduces incident shape. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Adversarial critique: if segmentation correctness only fails after hook payload normalization in another module, Unit could miss it. TOP2-CONTRAST runner-up Integration, but behavior text focuses on segmentation semantics only ("treats ... as separate segments"), which is a local function contract. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text does not require downstream classifier/state interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Call the segmentation function with a multiline block containing variable assignments and a later command; assert output contains distinct ordered segments per newline statement and never a single monolithic segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure boundary is segmenter -> trigger-classifier handoff; either module alone can look correct while composition fails. REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess required because behavior is detection/classification from command text, not executing `gh`. MOCK-HIDE: over-mocking classifier inputs could hide segmentation defects, so wire real local modules. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess execution, (b) no AI output dependence, (c) no agentic chain, (d) no LLM-governed handoff. Adversarial critique: if classifier is one pure function already consuming raw block, Unit might suffice. TOP2-CONTRAST runner-up Unit; final Integration wins because incident explicitly involved whole-block classification after multiline composition.
behaviors.test_description: Run hook detection pipeline end-to-end in-process (segment + classify) on a multiline command block with assignments then `gh pr create`; assert outcome is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: negative case depends on same segmenter->classifier interaction and token discrimination across segments. REAL-INFRA: no real `gh` execution needed; classification-only behavior. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing intermediate normalized segments can mask mis-segmentation leading to false positives. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess, (b) no AI output, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM-stage orchestration. Adversarial critique: if command matching happens in one isolated matcher, Unit could catch it. TOP2-CONTRAST runner-up Unit; final Integration wins because the bug class is "multi-line block misclassified," a composed-path failure.
behaviors.test_description: Execute in-process detection pipeline with multiline assignments + `gh issue create`; assert classification is non-PR trigger and specifically not PR-create.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at detector -> identifier reconstruction -> state persistence boundary. REAL-INFRA: requires local filesystem write/read path, but not real network/external subprocess per behavior text. MOCK-HIDE: mocking persistence would miss serialization/path bugs that drop identifier. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real external subprocess/network; local filesystem is sufficient at Integration, (b) no AI output, (c) no agentic chain, (d) no LLM orchestration. Adversarial critique: if persistence layer is thin and deterministic, Unit tests of serializer + writer may be enough. TOP2-CONTRAST runner-up System; final Integration wins because behavior text says reconstruct/persist state, not verify real `gh`/HTTP behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System since no text requiring real CLI/API call (only "reconstruct and persist").
behaviors.test_description: Using temporary local filesystem, invoke hook flow with a detected PR-create event carrying realistic command/context; assert persisted state contains a valid reconstructed PR identifier in expected key/format.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is an inter-module contract (post-tool writer -> pre-tool reader/enforcer); isolated unit checks can pass while schema/version mismatch breaks enforcement. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem contract is required; no external network/subprocess required by behavior text. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing file I/O or reader output can hide real incompatibility. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess beyond local file operations, (b) no AI output dependence, (c) no agentic sequence, (d) no LLM-governed stage handoff. Adversarial critique: if both hooks share a single typed state object in-process, Unit contract test might be enough. TOP2-CONTRAST runner-up System; final Integration wins because required boundary is local hooks + shared state format, not external infrastructure. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System since behavior text scopes to "written in a form ... can read and enforce," not runtime subprocess/network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run post-tool hook to write review-gate state after PR-create detection, then run pre-tool enforcement hook against that state file; assert enforcement path activates (or blocks) as expected using the persisted identifier.
